phrase.text: The weather changes with the <seasons> and <months>.
There are usually four <seasons> in a year: <spring>, <summer>, <autumn|fall> and <winter>.

When it is summer in the <northern hemisphere>, in Europe for example, it is winter in the <southern hemisphere>, in Australia and South America. When it is winter in Paris, the people in Rio de Janeiro celebrate Carnival outside in the streets.
In some <regions> of the world there is also a <dry season> and a <rainy season>.

It is <spring> in the Hel peninsula in Poland. The weather is <mild>. The <birds> are back after <winter>. It often rains and the grass is <green>. The leaves in the <trees> are green, too. The <lighthouse> is red. The <water> in the bay is <grey>. There are no <waves>. It is <calm>.

It is <summer> in Santa Cruz de Tenerife. The weather is <hot>. The sun <shines> every day. The <sky> is blue. The water in the <ocean> is blue, too. There are some clouds in the sky. The <temperature> of the air can be 30 or 35 degrees C. The days are long and the nights are short. The sun <rises> early and <sets> late.
It is <autumn> in Gdynia in Poland. There are <big> waves breaking on the <sandy> beach.
It is often <windy>. The Baltic sea can be <rough>. The sky is <overcast> with <clouds>.

It <rains> sometimes. The temperature of the air is about 15 degrees C. The leaves in the trees are turning <yellow> and <brown>. The season is called “the <fall>” in America because the leaves <fall> from the trees. The birds are preparing to <migrate> to warmer <regions> of the world.
It is <winter> in Jastarnia in the Hel peninsula in Poland. The weather is very <cold>. It often <snows>. There is sometimes <ice> in the bay. The temperature of the air can fall <below> zero.
The days are <short> and the nights are <long>. The sun rises late and sets early.
